text.title: Use Case Modeling — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of Use Case Modeling must enforce.

rule.statement: Every use case names actor + goal + scenario triplet.
rule.rationale: Use cases without explicit actor/goal collapse into feature descriptions that miss user intent.

rule.statement: Main success scenario is numbered steps with actor + system response per step.
rule.rationale: Free-form prose hides branching points; numbered steps make alternates and exceptions clear.

rule.statement: Every use case lists named preconditions that must hold before the scenario starts.
rule.rationale: Preconditions encode assumptions; uncatalogued assumptions surface as production defects.

rule.statement: Every use case includes at least one alternate flow (decision branch).
rule.rationale: Single-path use cases miss the variability that test design needs.

rule.statement: Every use case includes at least one exception flow (error path).
rule.rationale: Happy-path-only use cases mislead testers; exception flows are 60%+ of real-world test scope.
